intro: Hymenostephium cordatum is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Compositae (Asteraceae), native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hymenostephium cordatum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hymenostephium cordatum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its therapeutic potential and is often used to treat various conditions, including respiratory infections and skin irritations.
